title: Autonomous Decision Threshold Breach Monitoring (Art. 14)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for Art.13.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that EU AI Act Art.13 (Transparency and information) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.21.13: Autonomous Decision Threshold Breach Monitoring (Art. 14).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.21.13' running on index=ai_policy sourcetype=ai:decision:policy — risk_score, auto_approve, human_gate, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (2890)
spl:
index=ai_policy sourcetype="ai:decision:policy" earliest=-24h auto_approve=true
| lookup ai_risk_thresholds.csv model_id OUTPUT max_auto_risk
| eval breach=if(risk_score > max_auto_risk,1,0)
| where breach=1 OR (auto_approve=true AND human_gate=false AND risk_score>0.6)
| stats count by model_id, user_segment, breach
| sort - count
description: Alerts when decisions are auto-approved above configured risk_score without human gate, evidencing threshold enforcement.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for EU AI Act compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ai_policy and sourcetypes ai:decision:policy, ai:decision:policy`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Maintain thresholds lookup by jurisdiction; (2) correlate with credit/product policies; (3) notable in ES on breach; (4) monthly threshold review attestation. Indexes required: `index=ai_policy`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ai:decision:policy`, `sourcetype=ai:decision:policy``.
visualization: Time chart (breach count), Table (top model_id), Single value (breaches/1k decisions)
knownFalsePositives: High-risk system events during model retraining, scheduled validation runs, or feature rollouts that are pre-approved in the model risk register.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (2890).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ai_policy` `sourcetype=ai:decision:policy` — risk_score, auto_approve, human_gate.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain thresholds lookup by jurisdiction; (2) correlate with credit/product policies; (3) notable in ES on breach; (4) monthly threshold review attestation.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ai_policy sourcetype="ai:decision:policy" earliest=-24h auto_approve=true
| lookup ai_risk_thresholds.csv model_id OUTPUT max_auto_risk
| eval breach=if(risk_score > max_auto_risk,1,0)
| where breach=1 OR (auto_approve=true AND human_gate=false AND risk_score>0.6)
| stats count by model_id, user_segment, breach
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Autonomous Decision Threshold Breach Monitoring (Art. 14) (EU AI Act)** — Alerts when decisions are auto-approved above configured risk_score without human gate, evidencing threshold enforcement.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ai_policy` `sourcetype=ai:decision:policy` — risk_score, auto_approve, human_gate. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (2890). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ai_policy; **sourcetype**: ai:decision:policy. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ai_policy, sourcetype="ai:decision:policy", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **breach** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where breach=1 OR (auto_approve=true AND human_gate=false AND risk_score>0.6)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by model_id, user_segment, breach** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart (breach count), Table (top model_id), Single value (breaches/1k decisions)
grandmaExplanation: We alerts when decisions are auto-approved above configured risk_score without human gate, evidencing threshold enforcement.
